title: Perinatal Maternal Mental Health Detection And Care Access Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring perinatal maternal depression detection and care access gaps in low-income countries, linking DHS maternal-health data, mental-health service data, and community-health-worker data.
why_it_matters: WHO estimates 10-20 percent of women in LMICs experience perinatal depression, with detection rates below 10 percent and treatment rates near zero in most low-income countries. Maternal depression affects child growth, development, and survival, yet maternal mental health is systematically excluded from maternal health programs.

success_metrics.target: Every detection-gap estimate links survey, service, and workforce data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
